title: (quasiquote x) / (unquote x) long forms are not wired - only reader sugar ` and , work
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). The written-out forms are treated as plain data: (quasiquote (a (unquote (+ 1 1)) c)) -> (a (unquote (+ 1 1)) c) instead of (a 2 c); an (unquote ...) long form inside a sugared ` template is likewise left unsubstituted. R7RS 4.2.8: `x is (quasiquote x) and ,x is (unquote x) - all spellings must behave identically. JIT and AOT agree on the wrong value.
acceptance: tests/stress/found/quasiquote_long_form.esk prints OK quasiquote-long-form under -r and AOT
campaign_payoff: Code generators and macro-written code routinely emit the long forms; silently returning them as data corrupts output.
